question: Based on this scatterplot, what is the most reliable conclusion you can draw about the relationship between age and salary?

options.A: It is clear that individuals between ages 55 and 60 earn noticeably more than all other age groups.
options.B: It is impossible to identify any overall relationship between age and salary from this chart.
options.C: The chart suggests that salary generally increases with age, but the small plot makes detailed comparisons between close age groups unreliable.
options.D: Individuals between ages 50 and 55 clearly have the highest salaries among all age groups.

explanation: The individuals in the chart between the ages of 55 and 60 have the highest salary. The small size of the chart may cause difficulty in accurately identifying the specific trends of salary in certain age ranges.
